questionTypeDefinitions.classic-presentation: Textbook clinical scenario; tests whether learner recognizes the prototypical presentation and selects the correct diagnosis or next step
questionTypeDefinitions.atypical-presentation: Non-classic demographics or symptom pattern (elderly, pregnant, diabetic, immunocompromised, pediatric, opposite sex from classic); tests breadth of recognition
questionTypeDefinitions.next-best-step: Clinical scenario ends mid-workup or mid-management; learner must identify the single most appropriate immediate next action
questionTypeDefinitions.diagnostic-reasoning: Multiple diagnoses are plausible; question tests the distinguishing feature, confirmatory test, or risk stratification tool that differentiates them
questionTypeDefinitions.management-decision: Diagnosis is established; learner must choose the correct first-line therapy, drug class, monitoring parameter, or dose adjustment
questionTypeDefinitions.complication-recognition: Patient is already in treatment or has a known condition; learner must identify an emerging complication, drug adverse effect, or clinical deterioration
questionTypeDefinitions.contraindication: Learner must identify which drug, procedure, or management approach is contraindicated in the given patient context
questionTypeDefinitions.edge-case-expert: Competing priorities, resource constraints, special populations, ethical tension, or conflicting guidelines; requires expert-level clinical judgment synthesis

questionTypeAllocationTemplate.note: Tier-C-18q applies to narrow-scope topics. Some Tier-C topics are capped at 18 without duplication risk; see per-topic notes. A small set of complex topics are allocated 30-35 questions (high-exam-weight premium).
